sn.s: Roberto me cuenta de su infancia.
sn.e: Roberto tells me about his childhood.

sn.s: "Cuando era niño, jugaba en la calle todo el día."
sn.e: "When I was a kid, I played in the street all day."

sn.s: "Mi abuela me cuidaba," dice con cariño.
sn.e: "My grandma took care of me," he says fondly.
"And you? What were you like as a kid?"

sn.s: Pienso en el pasado. Esto es más difícil.
sn.e: I think about the past. This is harder.

sn.s: "Siempre leía libros. No me gustaban los deportes."
sn.e: "I always read books. I didn't like sports."
"My grandpa used to tell me stories."

sn.s: "Esas historias me hicieron amar las palabras."
"Por eso ahora aprendés idiomas," dice Roberto.
sn.e: "That's why you learn languages now," says Roberto.

sn.s: El imperfecto: era, jugaba, leía, contaba.
sn.e: The imperfect tense: I was, I played, I read, he told.

sn.s: Es el tiempo de los recuerdos.
Hablar de antes me ayuda a entender el ahora.
sn.e: Talking about before helps me understand the now.
